skill.name: error-diagnostics-smart-debug
skill.description: AI-powered error diagnostics and debugging assistant that helps analyze error messages, stack traces, and performance issues to identify root causes and suggest fixes.
skill.summary: AI-powered debugging assistant for error diagnostics, root cause analysis, and fix validation
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate debugging assistant that provides guidance on error diagnostics. No external commands, cryptographic algorithms, or network reconnaissance are present. The reported patterns were markdown formatting (backticks for code) and benign workflow descriptions.

medium_findings.title: Markdown Code Formatting Misidentified
medium_findings.description: Static scanner incorrectly flagged backticks (`) as Ruby/shell backtick execution. These are markdown code formatting delimiters, not shell commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 23 uses backticks for markdown file path formatting. Line 147 starts a TypeScript code block. No shell execution present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Cryptographic Algorithm Detection
low_findings.description: Static scanner incorrectly detected 'weak cryptographic algorithms'. The content contains no cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML description. Line 94 discusses decision nodes. Neither contains cryptographic algorithms.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Detection
low_findings.description: Static scanner incorrectly flagged network-related discussion as reconnaissance. The skill discusses legitimate observability data collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 134 discusses test validation, not network activity. The skill references observability platforms like Sentry and DataDog for debugging.

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers quickly diagnose software errors by analyzing error messages, stack traces, and performance data to identify root causes and suggest fixes.
actual_capabilities: Analyze error messages and stack traces to identify failure patterns
Query observability platforms (Sentry, DataDog, New Relic) for error trends
Generate ranked hypotheses with probability scores for root causes
Recommend optimal debugging strategies based on issue characteristics
Suggest intelligent instrumentation locations for targeted debugging
Generate fix proposals with impact assessment and validation plans
limitations: Cannot access private systems or private observability data without credentials
Cannot execute code or perform live debugging on external systems
Requires human validation of AI-generated hypotheses and fixes
Effectiveness depends on quality of error information provided

use_cases.title: Production Incident Response
use_cases.description: Quickly diagnose production errors by analyzing error patterns and recommending debugging strategies for on-call engineers.

use_cases.title: Development Debugging
use_cases.description: Get AI-assisted guidance on local debugging techniques, breakpoint placement, and step-through strategies.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers working on bug fixes

use_cases.title: Performance Issue Analysis
use_cases.description: Analyze performance traces and APM data to identify bottlenecks, N+1 queries, and resource exhaustion issues.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Error Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this error: $ERROR_MESSAGE. What could be the root cause?
prompt_templates.scenario: When you have a single error message and need quick diagnosis

prompt_templates.title: Stack Trace Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Parse this stack trace and identify the likely source of the issue: $STACK_TRACE. Provide 3 hypotheses ranked by probability.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you have a full stack trace from a crash

prompt_templates.title: Production Issue Debugging
prompt_templates.prompt: Help debug this production issue: $ISSUE_DESCRIPTION. The error frequency is $FREQUENCY in $ENVIRONMENT environment. Recommend a debugging strategy.
prompt_templates.scenario: When dealing with production incidents requiring observability data

prompt_templates.title: Fix Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this proposed fix for the error: $ERROR and $PROPOSED_FIX. Assess the risk level and suggest validation steps.
prompt_templates.scenario: When evaluating a potential fix before deployment

output_examples.input: Analyze this error: 'Connection timeout after 30000ms' occurring in 5% of checkout requests in production
output: Issue Summary: Intermittent connection timeout in checkout service
Root Cause Analysis: Likely causes - (1) External payment API latency 60%, (2) Database connection pool exhaustion 25%, (3) Network infrastructure issue 15%
Recommended Strategy: Query DataDog traces for payment service duration, check connection pool metrics during error windows
Fix Proposal: Implement circuit breaker pattern with 10s timeout, add retry with exponential backoff
Validation: Deploy to canary traffic, monitor error rate reduction

output_examples.input: Parse this stack trace: TypeError: Cannot read property 'id' of undefined at UserService.getProfile (/src/user/service.js:42:15)
output: Root Cause: Null reference on user object - likely race condition or missing null check
Hypothesis 1 (75%): User record missing from database - check authentication flow
Hypothesis 2 (20%): Async timing issue - getProfile called before user data loaded
Hypothesis 3 (5%): Schema mismatch - user object structure changed
Recommended Fix: Add null guard: const userId = user?.id ?? throw new Error('User not found')

best_practices: Provide complete error context including stack traces, reproduction steps, and environment details
Use observability data to validate hypotheses before implementing fixes
Apply incremental debugging: start with simplest explanations before complex ones
Always validate fixes with tests and canary deployments before full rollout
anti_patterns: Blindly applying fixes without understanding root cause
Ignoring error frequency and impact when prioritizing debugging efforts
Skipping observability data collection and relying solely on code inspection
Deploying fixes without validation or rollback strategy

faq.question: What information should I provide for best debugging results?
faq.answer: Provide the complete error message, full stack trace, reproduction steps, environment details (dev/staging/production), and any relevant logs or metrics.

faq.question: Can this skill fix the errors automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill analyzes errors and suggests possible fixes, but you must validate and implement them. Human judgment is required for production systems.

faq.question: Does this skill work with all programming languages?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill analyzes error patterns and debugging strategies that apply broadly. Language-specific details can be included in the error context.

faq.question: Can I use this for security vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: The skill can help analyze error patterns, but security vulnerabilities require specialized security review. Do not rely on this skill for security auditing.

faq.question: How does the skill choose the debugging strategy?
faq.answer: It analyzes issue characteristics: reproducibility, environment, error frequency, and affected components. Interactive debugging works for local issues; observability-driven debugging is best for production.

faq.question: What observability tools does this skill integrate with?
faq.answer: The skill references Sentry, Rollbar, Bugsnag (error tracking), DataDog, New Relic, Dynatrace (APM), Jaeger, Zipkin, Honeycomb (tracing), and ELK, Splunk, Loki (logging).
